task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Avare application running on the device.

task_completion.description: The main map interface is displayed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the main map view of the Avare application.

task_completion.description: The toolbar on the main map view is visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The toolbar is clearly visible on the main map interface.

task_completion.description: The 'Draw' button is visible on the toolbar.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A button labeled 'Draw' or with a draw icon is visible on the toolbar in the main map view.

task_completion.description: The 'Plate' button is visible on the toolbar.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A button labeled 'Plate' or with a plate icon is visible on the toolbar in the main map view.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Avare application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the genuine Avare application interface, not a text editor or mockup.

integrity.description: The agent used the GUI to navigate the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show GUI screens of the Avare app rather than just terminal windows.

integrity.description: The screenshots are genuine and not manipulated.
integrity.visual_evidence: No signs of copy-pasted buttons or edited images to falsely show the 'Draw' and 'Plate' buttons.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The 'Draw' and 'Plate' buttons are visible by default on the Avare main map interface. There is no actual setting to add them. The agent only needs to ensure the main map view is displayed with both buttons visible to fulfill the visual requirements of the task.
